data.title: Monster Beverage says Spruce Point short report 'false' and 'misleading'
data.description:
Monster Beverage (NASDAQ:MNST) said a short report from Spruce Point contains "false and misleading" characterizations of the company.

"The document released by a self-interested activist short seller is filled with and based on inaccuracies and aspersions that appear to be designed to distort the Company’s record and share price for its own gain," Monster Co-CEOs Rodney Sacks and Hilton Schlosberg said in a statement on Wednesday. 

The Monster Beverage (NASDAQ:MNST) response comes after the Spruce Point short report was issued on Tuesday. Shares of Monster rose 2% on Wednesday after falling 3.7% on Tuesday.

Monster (MNST) added that the short report doesn't have any credibility and the company doesn't plan to have any further comment.

data.title: SNDL to buy 32 stores from 1CM
data.description:
• SNDL (NASDAQ:SNDL) and 1CM (CSE: EPIC) signed a deal for SNDL to buy 32 cannabis retail stores operating under the Cost Cannabis and T Cannabis banners in Ontario, Alberta and Saskatchewan.
  • SNDL said it will acquire the 1CM Stores for a cash value of C$32.2M.
  • This acquisition is expected to bring SNDL's total owned and franchised cannabis retail store count to 219.
  • Deal is expected to close by the end of the third quarter of 2025, the company said.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow are mixed as China puts 84% tariffs on U.S. products
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages fell sharply on Wednesday as China reportedly slapped 84% tariffs on U.S. products after the Trump administration went ahead with its 104% levy on Chinese goods.

Early on and the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.1%, the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.5%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -0.6%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 9 basis points to 4.38%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 3.75%.

In response to the U.S.'s 104% tariffs on Chinese goods, Beijing will raise its tariff on U.S. goods to 84%, effective April 10, the government said on Wednesday, according to media reports.

Another topsy-turvy trading session on Wall Street saw the major averages rally furiously at the open, then steadily give up gains and turn negative, followed by a period of fluctuation. They then decidedly turned lower and eventually ended deep in the red.

"Several triggers drove a sharp turn lower in market sentiment as the day went on. First, we had confirmation from U.S. officials that the additional 50% tariff that Trump threatened China with on Monday would be imposed, dashing any remaining hopes for a last-minute extension before the deadline," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"The market turmoil has shown no sign of letting up over the last 24 hours, with the S&P 500 edging increasingly close to bear market territory, having now shed -18.9% since its peak in mid-February. Moreover, futures are still pointing towards further losses today,"  Allen said.

Trump on Tuesday also said that the U.S. will soon introduce a "major" tariff on pharmaceutical imports.

"Trump implemented an extraordinary tax increase on US consumers overnight. If the 104% tax increase on goods from China is not reversed, it is likely to push the US into recession more rapidly," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

For years, the U.S. persuaded foreigners to exchange U.S. government promises for valuable goods and services. That exchange is threatened—despite equities falling yesterday, bond yields rose sharply, Donovan added.

The results of $39B, 10-year note auction and the FOMC minutes will land later in the day on Wednesday.

data.title: Blackstone raises $10.6B of commitments for European real estate fund
data.description:
• Alternative asset manager Blackstone (NYSE:BX) announced on Wednesday the close of its latest European real estate fund, raising $10.6B of capital commitments.
  • Blackstone Real Estate Partners Europe VII has become the largest European real estate drawdown fund ever raised based on third party capital commitments.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Wednesday: DAL, WMT, LNC and F
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures on Wednesday recovered a chunk of their losses, but were still pointing to a negative open. Tariffs continued to be top of investors' minds after another retaliation from China. Here are some stocks to watch on Wednesday: 

  • Shares of Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) were +3.2% in pre-market trading. The number one U.S. carrier comfortably beat Q1 revenue expectations by over half a billion dollars, helped by strong demand from premium and international travelers. However, the airline pulled its full-year guidance, citing "broad economic uncertainty around global trade" that had stalled growth. See how DAL performed in charts. 
  • Walmart (NYSE:WMT) stock was +0.3% ahead of the opening bell. The U.S. retail giant at an investor meeting reaffirmed its Q1 sales growth forecast of 3% to 4% and stuck to its annual sales and operating income growth guidance. "The range of outcomes for Q1 operating income growth has widened due to less favorable category mix, higher casualty claims expense, and the desire to maintain flexibility to invest in price as tariffs are implemented," WMT said.
  • Shares of Lincoln Financial (NYSE:LNC) were +7.2% ahead of market open, after it said private equity firm Bain Capital will acquire a 9.9% ownership stake for $825M. As part of the deal, Lincoln (NYSE:LNC) and Bain Capital have also agreed to enter into a 10-year, non-exclusive strategic investment management relationship. LNC will sell approximately 18.8M shares of its common stock for $44/share.
  • Ford (NYSE:F) stock was -1.2% in pre-market trading. Bernstein downgraded its rating on the stock to Underperform from Market Perform, citing significant downside "not priced by the market yet" around tariffs. "We account for tariff costs, price hikes, market share gains, and a slight decline in consumer sentiment, reducing auto free cash flow by $6.7 billion from 2025 to 2027," the brokerage predicted.

data.title: Osisko reports Q1 gold output, quarterly cash margin
data.description:
• Osisko Gold Royalties (NYSE:OR) on Wednesday said that it earned 19,014 attributable gold equivalent ounces in the first quarter.
  • Osisko recorded preliminary revenues from royalties and streams of $54.9 million during the first quarter and preliminary cost of sales (excluding depletion) of $1.6 million, resulting in a quarterly cash margin of approximately $53.3 million.
  • As at March 31, Osisko’s cash position was approximately $63.1 million, following a $19.6 million net repayment on the company’s revolving credit facility during the first quarter.
  • Subsequent to quarter end, Osisko (NYSE:OR) paid down an additional $25.0 million against its revolving credit facility, reducing the outstanding balance to $49.3 million.

data.title: Aegon to reset coupon on subordinated bonds
data.description:
• Dutch insurer Aegon (NYSE:AEG) announced on Wednesday the reset of coupon on its EUR 113M 1.506% perpetual cumulative subordinated bonds.
  • The reset is effective on June 8, with the new coupon to be published on or around June 3.

data.title: Six Flags Entertainment is called undervalued by UBS
data.description:
UBS initiated coverage on Six Flags Entertainment Corporation (SIX) on Wednesday with a Buy rating.

Analyst Arpine Kocharyan said the key to the firm's investment thesis on Six Flags (NYSE:FUN) is that the theme park operator is likely to beat both earnings estimates, with a growth profile that could support re-rating of multiple closer to historical average of around 8.5X or higher due to upside from merger synergies.

"Specifically, we see $125-$130M upside to announced $80M of revenue synergies which do not take into account visitation recovery at Six Flags driven by an increase in season pass holder frequency of visits, as well as incremental revenue opportunities from price parity of season passes between Cedar and legacy Six. We believe Six Flag's shares are undervalued with a compelling risk/reward ratio of almost 3 to 1, despite a more uncertain economic backdrop."

On an important note, Kocharyan highlighted that the theme park industry remains more macro resilient as a more affordable out-of-home entertainment option, especially amid inflationary fears.

UBS set a price target of $49 on Six Flags (NYSE:FUN) to represent more than 60% upside for shares.

Six Flags Entertainment (SIX) was down 0.9% in premarket trading to $28.10. Short interest on SIX stands at 7.6% of the total float.

data.title: Commerce Resources, Mont Royal Resources to create Canadian-focused critical minerals development company
data.description:
• Commerce Resources (OTCQX:CMRZF) on Wednesday announced that it has entered into a definitive arrangement agreement with Mont Royal Resources to combine their respective businesses in a merger transaction.
  • Pursuant to the agreement, Mont Royal has agreed to acquire 100% of the issued and outstanding common shares of Commerce Resources by way of a court approved plan of arrangement under the Business Corporation Act.
  • The company said the transaction will create a Québec-focused critical minerals explorer and developer through the combination of Commerce's Ashram Rare Earths and Fluorspar Project and Eldor Niobium exploration project and Mont Royal's Northern Lights Lithium Project.
  • The merger will result in a dual listing on the TSX Venture Exchange and the Australian Securities Exchange, added the company.
  • Pursuant to the terms and conditions of the agreement, Commerce shareholders will receive 2.3271 fully-paid Mont Royal Shares for each Commerce Share held immediately prior to the effective time, implying a consideration of C$0.093 per Commerce Share.
  • The transaction is conditional upon, amongst other things, Mont Royal raising up to A$10.0 million through the issue of Mont Royal Shares pursuant to a public equity offering in Australia.
  • The net proceeds of the Mont Royal Equity Raise are expected to be used for advancing the Preliminary Economic Assessment ("PEA") at the Ashram Project, while also advancing the Eldor Niobium Project and the exploration pipeline at the Northern Lights Project.
  • In addition to the announcement of the transaction, Commerce intends to conduct a convertible note financing with a number of existing Commerce and Mont Royal shareholders and other sophisticated investors to raise up to C$2.2 million to provide interim funding to be used for the continuation of studies for the development of the Ashram Project and for working capital while the transaction is completed.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Casella Waste Systems rated Buy in new coverage at TD Cowen
data.description:
Casella Waste Systems (NASDAQ:CWST) on Wednesday was rated Buy in new research coverage by TD Cowen, which pointed to the company’s durable free cash flow, strong pricing power and upside to landfill volume growth as key drivers of future performance.

James Schumm, a research analyst at TD Cowen, noted that Casella’s (NASDAQ:CWST) resilience across economic cycles makes it a standout in the solid waste sector.

“M&A growth and free-cash-flow durability in good or bad economic times makes Casella a particularly attractive investment,” Schumm said.

Tariff impact limited, inflation could play to Casella’s strengths

Despite rising concerns around potential tariff-related cost pressures, Schumm sees minimal risk to Casella’s (NASDAQ:CWST) core operations.

“We think tariffs will have limited impact on the solid waste industry,” he said. “But if tariffs spur a wave of inflation, we think Casella is likely the most insulated, as we estimate roughly 75% of their business is not price-restricted and can increase prices immediately.”

Landfill volumes set for a comeback

After years of stagnation, landfill volumes may finally be on the upswing.

“Landfill revenues have been virtually flat since 2019 despite annual price growth of 6%–7%,” Schumm said.

But a competing Long Island landfill’s recent closure may reverse that trend, potentially pushing Casella’s (CWST) landfill volumes up 18% over time, with an approximately 8% boost expected in 2025.

Conservative 2025 guidance leaves room for upside

Casella’s (CWST) recently issued 2025 guidance was labeled “peer leading” by Schumm, but he believes it understates the company’s true potential.

“We think 2025 revenues have the potential to be 500 basis points higher than the initial guidance,” he said, suggesting this could translate into an approximately 600-basis-point beat on earnings before interest taxes, interest, depreciation and amortization when accounting for operating leverage and a more favorable mix.

Focus on free cash flow over margins

In evaluating performance drivers for solid waste firms, TD Cowen prioritizes free cash flow per share and margin expansion for earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization. Casella (CWST), Schumm said, is likely to outperform peers in free-cash-flow growth, although its ability to close the margin gap remains a key question given the margin dilution from its aggressive M&A strategy.

Margin expansion catalysts on the horizon

TD Cowen sees a clear path to margin improvement in 2025, estimating a $20 million ebitda uplift even before any price-driven growth. This includes:

  • $10–11 million from rising landfill volumes,
  • $5 million from truck fleet automation efforts,
  • $4 million from upgrades at the Willimantic recycling facility.

Valuation and outlook

TD Cowen set a price target of $130 a share on Casella (CWST), implying about 25% upside from current levels.

Casella (CWST) currently trades at 17 times and 15 times 2025 and 2026 enterprise-value-to-ebitda multiples, respectively — levels consistent with its five-year average. TD Cowen’s discounted-cash-flow-based price target of $130, using a 7.5% weighted average cost of capital, reflects confidence in the company’s long-term fundamentals and upside potential.

“With strong FCF growth, limited exposure to tariffs and several margin tailwinds on the horizon, Casella is well-positioned to outperform,” Schumm said.
